content: Warren env vars for reaching burrow are warren-namespaced: WARREN_BURROW_SOCKET (default /var/run/burrow.sock), WARREN_BURROW_HOST + WARREN_BURROW_PORT for TCP, WARREN_BURROW_TOKEN for cross-container deploys where burrow runs with --auth. Namespacing means a sibling process running 'burrow' directly can keep its own BURROW_API_TOKEN / BURROW_COLOR without collision. Loaded once at startup via loadBurrowClientConfigFromEnv() in src/burrow-client/config.ts.

name: transport-error vs server-error mapping
description: Calls into HttpClient throw two flavors: structured BurrowError subclasses (rehydrated from burrow's {error: {code, message, hint}} envelope, see burrow lib/http-client.ts rehydrateError) AND raw fetch failures (TypeError: fetch failed with .cause.code = ENOENT/ECONNREFUSED/etc). The facade's withTransportMapping() and BurrowClient.probe() convert ONLY the second category into BurrowUnreachableError (a WarrenError subclass), letting BurrowError flow through untouched so callers can keep using instanceof NotFoundError / ValidationError as documented in burrow's lib. Detection walks the prototype chain because burrow subclasses override .name rather than .constructor.name. Warren's HTTP server should map BurrowUnreachableError to 503 and pass BurrowError envelopes through to the client.

name: HttpClient surface to wrap
description: @os-eco/burrow-cli exports HttpClient (from src/lib/http-client.ts) with five namespaces: burrows (up/list/get/tryGet/stop/resume/destroy), runs (create/get/tryGet/list/cancel/delete/stream), inbox (send/list/pending/cancel/count), events (tail/replay), agents (list/get/require/has). Plus top-level healthz() (auth-exempt). Construction is sync — Transport union is { kind: 'unix', path } | { kind: 'tcp', hostname, port }. For testing, fetch override is the boundary stub: HttpClientOptions.fetch = (input, init) => Promise<Response>. Don't reimplement these surfaces in warren — wrap and forward.

content: BurrowClientPool (src/burrow-client/pool.ts, warren-41a2 / pl-9ba1 step 3) is the multi-worker successor to BurrowClient.fromEnv(). Holds Map<workerName, BurrowClient>; exposes placeFor({projectId}) (delegates to placeForProject) and clientFor({burrowId}) (delegates to placeForBurrow) returning {workerName, client}. fromEnv({env, repos, fetch?, now?}) is the zero-config boot: synthesizes a 'local' worker row (upsert preserves existing state - probe-derived state wins on re-boot), constructs one BurrowClient from WARREN_BURROW_* env vars, registers under LOCAL_WORKER_NAME='local'. pool.singleton() is back-compat scaffolding bootServer uses for the legacy burrowClient variable until pl-9ba1 steps 4+5 migrate spawnRun/bridges/scheduler off it; throws ValidationError if size!==1. transportToUrl renders unix:// or http://host:port onto workers.url for operator diagnostics. probe() is allSettled per-worker returning ProbeResult[] with ok/error rather than throwing.

name: fanOutAcrossWorkers helper
description: src/burrow-client/fanout.ts (warren-14ad / pl-9ba1 step 5) is the Promise.allSettled fan-out helper for reads that are not scoped to a single burrow. Signature: fanOutAcrossWorkers(pool, fn(client, workerName), { logger, op }) → { results: [{workerName, value}], errors: [{workerName, error}] }. Iterates pool.names() (alphabetical) so result arrays are deterministic; callers sort downstream by domain field (e.g. createdAt). Per-worker rejections are normalized to Error and emitted as a worker_unreachable warn line via the optional logger; the helper never throws. Per-resource reads (burrows.get(id), runs.get(id), streaming) deliberately do NOT fan out — they use pool.clientFor({burrowId}) so the request goes to exactly the worker that holds the burrow's sandbox + SQLite row (sticky-by-burrow).

name: BurrowClient.setDrain admin shim
description: BurrowClient.setDrain(drain) (src/burrow-client/client.ts) hand-rolls POST /admin/drain because @os-eco/burrow-cli's HttpClient does not expose the admin namespace. Reuses the same transport (unix socket via init.unix, TCP via http://host:port), same bearer auth header, and runs the call through withTransportMapping so transport-layer failures lift to BurrowUnreachableError. Non-2xx envelopes pass through rehydrateAdminError which rebuilds BurrowError subclasses (NotFoundError / ValidationError / CredentialError) and falls back to HttpClientError for unmapped codes, mirroring what HttpClient's private rehydrateError does in burrow-cli.

content: BurrowClientPool.fromConfig (warren-272c) is the multi-worker successor to fromEnv: takes ParsedWorkerEntry[] (name + url + parsed Transport) + a shared bearer token, upserts each row into workers, registers a BurrowClient per entry. Does NOT synthesize the 'local' worker — [workers] config defines the complete pool. Throws ValidationError on empty input (caller should pick fromEnv for the zero-config path). bootServer picks the factory based on loaded fileConfig.workers.length.
